title: asclepias humistrata
intro: Asclepias humistrata, also known as Pineleaf Milkweed, is a perennial plant native to the southeastern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever reduction, coughs, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant properties and ability to reduce inflammation. Asclepias humistrata can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
